content.problemStatement:
Vivek is quite fond of expected values. One day, he stumbled upon the following problem. He cannot solve it, so he is asking you for help.

Consider all trees with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN); two trees are different if there is a pair of vertices uuu and vvv such that there is an edge between vertices uuu and vvv in exactly one of these trees.

For a uniformly randomly chosen tree TTT, let's denote the degrees of vertices 111 through NNN in this tree by d1,d2,‚Ä¶,dNd_1, d_2, \ldots, d_Nd1‚Äč,d2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,dN‚Äč. Then, let's denote A=(d1‚čÖd2‚čÖ‚Ä¶‚čÖdN)KA = (d_1 \cdot d_2 \cdot \ldots \cdot d_N)^KA=(d1‚Äč‚čÖd2‚Äč‚čÖ‚Ä¶‚čÖdN‚Äč)K. Find the expected value of AAA.

It can be proved that the expected value of AAA can be expressed as a fraction P/QP/QP/Q, where PPP and QQQ are coprime positive integers and QQQ is coprime to 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353. You should compute the value of P‚čÖQ‚ąí1P \cdot Q^{-1}P‚čÖQ‚ąí1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353, where Q‚ąí1Q^{-1}Q‚ąí1 denotes the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2,000,0002,000,0002,000,000
**Subtask #2 (30 points):** the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 100,000100,000100,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē P‚čÖQ‚ąí1(mod998244353)P \cdot Q^{-1} \pmod{998244353}P‚čÖQ‚ąí1(mod998244353).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There are 333 labelled trees with size 333: the paths [1‚ąí2‚ąí3][1-2-3][1‚ąí2‚ąí3], [1‚ąí3‚ąí2][1-3-2][1‚ąí3‚ąí2] and [3‚ąí1‚ąí2][3-1-2][3‚ąí1‚ąí2].
The expected value is ((1‚čÖ2‚čÖ1)1+(1‚čÖ1‚čÖ2)1+(2‚čÖ1‚čÖ1)1)/3=2\left((1 \cdot 2 \cdot 1)^1 + (1 \cdot 1 \cdot 2)^1 + (2 \cdot 1 \cdot 1)^1\right) / 3 = 2((1‚čÖ2‚čÖ1)1+(1‚čÖ1‚čÖ2)1+(2‚čÖ1‚čÖ1)1)/3=2, so P=2P = 2P=2, Q=1Q = 1Q=1, Q‚ąí1=1Q^{-1} = 1Q‚ąí1=1 and the answer is 222.
